files.data:
# Image and research sources

Ancient Egypt uses locally bundled historical images. The source links below correspond to the lesson images and further reading.

- **The Nile’s Rhythm** — image: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Special:FilePath/Nile_River_and_Delta_from_orbit.jpg; research source: https://www.britannica.com/place/Nile-River
- **A World of Writing** — image: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Special:FilePath/Rosetta_Stone.JPG; research source: https://www.metmuseum.org/essays/papyrus-in-ancient-egypt
- **Pharaoh and State** — image: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Special:FilePath/Colossi_of_Memnon_2017.jpg; research source: https://www.worldhistory.org/Egyptian_Government/
- **Daily Life and Work** — image: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Special:FilePath/Model_of_bakery_and_brewery_from_the_tomb_of_Meketre.jpg; research source: https://www.worldhistory.org/article/933/daily-life-in-ancient-egypt/
- **Preparing for the Afterlife** — image: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Special:FilePath/Book_of_the_Dead_of_Hunefer_cropped_1.jpg; research source: https://www.metmuseum.org/art/collection/search/557590
- **How Egypt Is Remembered** — image: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Special:FilePath/Temple_of_Hatshepsut_2010.jpg; research source: https://www.metmuseum.org/toah/hd/egyp/hd_egyp.htm
